Video.summary: Time-saving quick tips to feed your crafting craving.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Learn to sew, step-by-step with National Sewing Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.grandparentTitle: National Sewing Circle presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: George Vondriska and Matt Newborg work together to share some of their best sanding techniques that are guaranteed to save you time in the shop. Find out which sanding techniques work best with different types of wood, learn the difference between various types of sandpaper, and see which tools are available for your sanding needs.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Choosing the Right Sanding Method

Video.summary: Great cuts make for more accurate quilts, and Angela is here to share some tips and tricks for quick and precise fabric cutting. Plus, she shares the best ruler sizes to have on hand.
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Creative Crochet Corner is an online video streaming service all about learning how to crochet! Whether you're a new to crochet just learning the basics or an experienced crocheter looking for a challenge, Creative Crochet Corner is here to help. We offer a vast library of video tutorials taught by talented crochet experts who guide you through each technique step-by-step, sharing their best tips and tricks.
Video.grandparentTitle: Creative Crochet Corner presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Episode 1

Video.summary: Just step outside to find artsy inspiration in nature. Calligrapher Janet Takahashi adds floral accents to plain lettering. Metalsmith Mary Hettmansperger adds patinas to copper. Art entrepreneur Jenn Mason captures nature's beauty in mono-stenciling. Julie Fei Fan Balzer has a tip for printing designs with items from nature.
Video.title: Nature

Video.summary: As the 1920's roared in, women bared their shoulders and danced to the latest jazz. Angela Wolf creates a cutwork tank top using today's computer software and digital cutter. Amber Eden adds color and detail to a classic black sheath dress with silk flower embroidery.
Video.title: All that Jazz - 1920's

Video.summary: Bruce Kieffer joins George Vondriska in the workshop to share some game-changing tips to make your life easier in the shop. From squaring your drill press with a wire hanger to getting the most precise reading from your miter gauge, follow along as Bruce and George spill their favorite shop secrets that will change the way you work.
Video.grandparentTitle: Woodworkers Guild of America presented by CraftsyTV
Video.title: Woodworking Tricks of the Trade

Video.summary: Mix things up with new shapes, colors and stitches. Modern quilter Riane Menardi modifies traditional blocks by changing colors and placement. Free-motion quilt artist Christina Cameli uses the walking foot to create spirals. Using her engineering background, quilt designer Yvonne Fuchs creates beautiful quilts with quarter square triangles.
Video.title: Mix It Up

Video.summary: From cornstarch to cuticle pushers, Angela shares five ingenious hacks for using common household items for quilting.
Video.title: Bonus: 5 Items Around Your House to Use for Quilting

Image.alt: Bonus: 5 Items Around Your House to Use for Quilting
Bonus: 5 Items Around Your House to Use for Quilting
Bonus: 5 Items Around Your House to Use for Quilting

Video.summary: Are you an organized quilter or a bit on the messier side like Angela? Watch as she uses solid colors to make a tried-and-true rainbow quilt by Monique Jacobs, sharing her best organizing tips along the way.
Video.title: Tried and True Rainbow Quilt

Video.summary: This week the Midnight Quilter is busy assembling Easter baskets, and a quilt, of course. See Angela demonstrate how to do a mitered border, plus how to quilt using rulers!
Video.title: Split Square

Video.summary: Ready to find your center? Tonight, Angela becomes one with the piecing as she makes a stress-relieving half-square triangle quilt.
Video.title: Stress Relieving Half-Square Triangle Quilt

Video.summary: WARNING: The following content depicts excessive charm square usage and may be too exciting for most quilters. In other words, Angela's ready to take on 200+ precuts, and the accompanying placement decisions.
Video.title: Star Squared

Video.summary: Are you Quilt Curious, but unsure where to get started? THIS is the Midnight Quilt Show episode for you!
Video.title: Friendship Stars Quilt

Video.summary: Angela’s not the only Midnight Quilter in the Walters' house! Watch as she and her teenage daughter Cloe spend some quality time together making an easy-to-piece charm quilt.
Video.title: Like a Charm

Video.summary: Are you cut out for paper piecing? Join Angela and find out as she avoids ripping paper, her kids and chores to create a super cool rounded illusion, no curves required.
Video.title: Rising Star

Video.summary: What happens when a rail fence meets a log cabin? Angela Walters' modern, lush batik quilt, demonstrated here!
Video.title: Rail Fence

Video.summary: Sleeves became important as women spent more time outdoors and synthetic fabrics became popular. Angela Wolf shows how to change a pattern to add batwing sleeves. Then she shows how to create bishop sleeves, inspired by eastern European folkwear.
Video.title: Fashionable Sleeves - Late 1920's

Video.summary: Start a color story with birthstones. Blogger and jewelry-maker Molly Schaller finds inspiration and meaning in birthstone colors. She also shares some great gift packaging ideas for those special jewelry gifts. Katie uses leather belting lace and crystal charms to make box braid bracelets in her Beading Lesson.
Video.title: The Color Story Begins with Birthstones

Image.alt: The Color Story Begins with Birthstones
The Color Story Begins with Birthstones
The Color Story Begins with Birthstones

Video.summary: Brenda Schweder is an expert on using jigs for forming wire. She will demonstrate how to use a jig, plus new ways to create buckle findings. Katie's Beading Lesson is a stylish color block necklace.
Video.title: Using Jigs for Wire Forming

Video.summary: Link filigree and pendants in creative ways. Learn to flat crimp a multi strand bracelet with Candie Cooper, make a square link to link bracelet with Patti Bullard and a draped necklace using premade chain -- whether crimping, wrapping; link your jewelry together with these creative ideas.
Video.title: Link It

Video.summary: The main reason we scrapbook is to capture those individual moments in time and keep them forever. Capture a moment in time in a scrapbook page, learn heat embossing with Kelly Purkey, and make a project life double page spread featuring a week in the life of Ali Edwards. A mix of ideas, supplies and designs - all in one Scrapbook Soup!
Video.title: A Day in the Life

Video.summary: Guests on this show share ways to celebrate friends and family. First, Kazan Clark creates a magnet board with bottlecaps. Next, Julie interviews Dina Wakley. Then, Stacy Julian shows how to make a family tree album. Last, Julie makes pop up place cards.
Video.title: Episode 9: All in the Family

Video.summary: Color adds impact to your designs. Host Julie Fei Fan Balzer adds color to dark backgrounds. Color explodes from collaged bags from Crafty Chica Kathy Cano-Murillo. Designer Mou Saha transforms a coloring book page into a watercolor painting. In the tip, mixed media artist Dina Wakley pours onto her journal pages.
Video.title: Color

Video.summary: Learn to bake and cook with CraftsyTV.
Video.title: Baking & Cooking

Video.summary: Cozy is calling! Join the ladies in an ode to warm projects, winter stews and all things bulky.
Video.title: Why We Love Cold Weather Knitting Projects

Image.alt: Why We Love Cold Weather Knitting Projects
Why We Love Cold Weather Knitting Projects
Why We Love Cold Weather Knitting Projects

Video.summary: Friends, snacks and yarn: what could be better? Bust your stash in the funnest way possible while making friends and scouting skeins.
Video.title: How to Throw an Unforgettable Yarn Swap Party

Image.alt: How to Throw an Unforgettable Yarn Swap Party
How to Throw an Unforgettable Yarn Swap Party
How to Throw an Unforgettable Yarn Swap Party

Video.summary: Whether indoors or out, games are fun! Bowl at home with family and friends with a colorful bowling set Katie makes from recycled water bottles. Franz shows how to create your own game board using a variety of art mediums. Jenny and Katie make an indoor hopscotch court that rolls-up, for easy storage. Braid a ring toss toy then use the Family Craft box to make a treasure hunt jar for quiet fun.
Video.title: Let's Play a Game

Video.summary: Welcome to CraftsyTV's Cook & Create, where delectable delights meet crafty creators.
Video.title: Cook & Create

Video.summary: Welcome to Artists's Academy where we are dedicated to making you a better artist. We believe everyone has an artist within, waiting to be unleashed. Our mission is to be your ultimate resource for visual art education using tutorials covering everything from fundamental techniques to advanced concepts in painting and drawing.
Video.title: Artist's Academy presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to knit, step-by-step with The Knitting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV
The Knitting Circle Presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: Learn to quilt step by step with National Quilting Circle experts on CraftsyTV.
Video.title: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Image.alt: National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV
National Quilting Circle presented by CraftsyTV

Video.summary: What do you do when your daughter picks out a quilt that's way harder than you're used to? Well if you're like Angela, you make an episode about it! This one's all about building up the confidence to take a bite out of something new. (You can do it too!)
Video.title: Episode 1
